prompt: Name one commonly-praised idea, trend, or practice in modern knowledge work that you think is overrated, and explain why.
response:
One commonly-praised practice in modern knowledge work that I believe is overrated is the emphasis on constant, real-time communication and collaboration tools like Slack or Microsoft Teams notifications.

While staying connected with colleagues is important, the expectation of immediate responses and constant availability can be disruptive to deep, focused work. The frequent context switching between tasks diminishes productivity and creativity.

Additionally, an overreliance on these tools can lead to a culture of performative busyness - constantly communicating but not necessarily producing meaningful work. The pressure to always appear available distracts from the quality of actual output.

Used judiciously, real-time collaboration tools have their place. But I believe their benefit is overstated and they are too often used in ways that fracture attention and elevate responsiveness over tangible results. More space for uninterrupted work can boost creativity and quality of output in knowledge work.
